target.name: U.S. National Science Foundation
target.notes_excerpt: Federal agency funding fundamental AI research and workforce development. Leads the NAIRR pilot connecting 600+ research teams to national AI infrastructure and invested $100M in National AI Research Institutes in 2025. Mission reoriented by Trump administration toward AI and quantum as top priorities.

source.name: Arati Prabhakar
source.notes_excerpt: Arati Prabhakar served as the 12th director of the White House Office of Science and Technology Policy from October 3, 2022 to January 20, 2025, and was the Science and Technology Advisor to President Biden. She was the first woman and person of color to be confirmed by the U.S. Senate as the Director of the White House Office of Science and Technology Policy. She was instrumental in passing Presi

target.name: National Institute of Standards and Technology
target.notes_excerpt: NIST develops voluntary AI standards and risk management frameworks for federal agencies and industry. Its AI Risk Management Framework (AI RMF 1.0) is widely adopted. In 2025-26 its AI Safety Institute was renamed the Center for AI Standards and Innovation (CAISI) under the Trump administration's AI Action Plan, with a mandate to revise the AI RMF.

source.name: Suresh Venkatasubramanian
source.notes_excerpt: Suresh Venkatasubramanian is a Professor of Data Science and Computer Science at Brown University who directs the Center for Technological Responsibility, Reimagination and Redesign. He served as Assistant Director for Science and Justice in the White House Office of Science and Technology Policy for the 2021-2022 academic year, where he co-authored the Blueprint for an AI Bill of Rights. target.name: University of Utah
target.notes_excerpt: University of Utah hosts the One-U Responsible AI Initiative, a $100M transdisciplinary program led by the Scientific Computing and Imaging (SCI) Institute. A $50M AI supercomputer partnership with NVIDIA and HPE is slated to come online in summer 2026. Research focuses on healthcare, societal wellness, and public services.

evidence: Backfilled from notes: ...Executive Director of Eleos AI. PhD Philosophy from NYU. Former resear

source.name: Robert Long
source.notes_excerpt: Executive Director of Eleos AI. PhD Philosophy from NYU. Former research fellow at Future of Humanity Institute (Oxford) and CAIS Philosophy Fellow (2023). Leading researcher on AI consciousness, sentience, and moral status. Co-author of "Consciousness in Artificial Intelligence" with Butlin, Bengio, et al. NOTE: Same person as Rob Long (entity 1354) — "Rob" is informal name.

target.name: Eleos AI
target.notes_excerpt: Nonprofit research org focused on AI welfare, consciousness, and moral patienthood. Co-founded by Kyle Fish, Robert Long, Kathleen Finlinson. Published landmark report "Taking AI Welfare Seriously." Commended Anthropic's model welfare efforts. Five research priorities: welfare interventions, human-AI cooperation, leveraging AI for welfare research, standardized evaluations, credible communication.

target.name: Cornell University
target.notes_excerpt: Major research university with broad AI activities spanning Cornell Tech (NYC), the AI Initiative, and the MacArthur-funded AI, Policy, and Practice (AIPP) initiative focused on AI's societal impacts. source.name: Yann LeCun
source.notes_excerpt: Yann LeCun is Chief AI Scientist at Meta and Silver Professor at New York University affiliated with the NYU Center for Data Science and the Courant Institute of Mathematical Sciences. He received the 2018 ACM Turing Award shared with Geoffrey Hinton and Yoshua Bengio for contributions to deep learning. target.name: Global AI Frontier Lab
target.notes_excerpt: Joint U.S.-South Korea AI research center at NYU, established May 2024 via MOU between NYU and Korea's IITP. Co-directed by Yann LeCun and Kyunghyun Cho. Focuses on three pillars: fundamental AI research, trustworthy/responsible AI, and AI for healthcare. Korean government investing ~$34M (45B won) with NYU contributing ~$31M by 2028. Opened September 2024 at One Metrotech Center, Brooklyn.

target.name: Asteria Film Co.
target.notes_excerpt: Asteria Film Co. is actually an AI-powered film production studio, not a traditional media/journalism organization. Founded by Bryn Mooser and Natasha Lyonne, it focuses on creating 'ethical AI' models for film production while developing the Continuum Suite operating system for streamlined filmmaking workflows. Backed by CAA and Comcast Ventures.

source.name: Ana Helena Ulbrich

Co-founded NoHarm in 2019 as a nonprofit AI tool that reviews medical prescriptions and flags potential dangers, now used by 200+ Brazilian hospitals analyzing 5 million prescriptions monthly. target.name: NoHarm

target.name: International Association for Safe and Ethical AI
target.notes_excerpt: The International Association for Safe and Ethical AI (IASEAI) is a non-profit organization founded in 2024 with headquarters in Paris, France. The organization's stated mission is to address the risks and opportunities associated with advances in artificial intelligence and to ensure that AI systems operate safely and ethically, benefiting all of humanity.
